free_text: Measurements of charge distributions of the fragments
in the low energy fission reaction
Detector system consisting of a
grid ionization chamber and a Delta-E-E particle
telescope, in which a thin grid ionization chamber
served as the Delta-E section and the E section was
an Au-Si surface barrier detector.
A Cf252 foil fission source with an activity of about
3000 fissions/s.
The typical physical quantities of fragments,
such as mass number and kinetic energies as well as
the deposition in the gas Delta-E detector and E
detector were derived from the coincident measurement
data.
The charge distributions of the light fragments for
the fixed mass number A and total kinetic energy were
obtained by the least-squares fits for the response
functions of the Delta-E detector with multi-Gaussian
functions representing the different elements.
The errors of the parameters are due to
the statistics errors of the fits of the Gaussian
distributions.
Compiled by WANG Jimin (CNDC) .
WANG Jimin (CNDC). J,PR/C,96,034611,2017
was added. EN-SEC, ERR-ANALYS and STATUS were moved
to SUBENT 001. SUBENT 002 was revised and SUBENT 003
was added.
Most probable charge at a given kinetic energy
Root-mean-square of charge disribution
Data were obtained from Hongyin Han.
WANG Jimin (CNDC). Root-mean-square of
charge disribution were added.
Fractional independent yield at a given kinetic energy
Data were obtained from Taofeng Wang.
WANG Jimin (CNDC).
